input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Prerequisites

| Tool | Source | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| **Xcode** | Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/) | iOS SDK, simulators, build tools |
| **Xcode Command Line Tools** | `xcode-select --install` | Compilers, git, etc. |
| **JDK 17** | Installed automatically by MAUI workload (Microsoft OpenJDK) | Android build toolchain |

Accept the Xcode license after install: `sudo xcodebuild -license accept`

---

## 2. Install .NET SDK

**Source:** [https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download)

- Download the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest) — pick the **macOS arm64** or **x64** installer matching your Mac.
- Discover current versions at the download page or via `dotnet --list-sdks` after install.

---

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

This pulls in everything: MAUI, Android SDK, Android build tools, Microsoft OpenJDK 17, and iOS/macOS bindings.

**Discover what was installed:**
```bash
dotnet workload list
```

---

## 4. Android SDK Details

The `maui` workload auto-installs the Android SDK to `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx`. If you need to manage it manually:

- **Android Studio (optional):** [https://developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) — useful for emulator management and SDK Manager GUI.
- The workload handles API levels automatically; check which with:
  ```bash
  dotnet android sdk list
  ```

---

## 5. IDE — Pick One

| IDE | Source | Notes |
|-----|--------|-------|
| **Visual Studio Code + C# Dev Kit** | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com/) + Extensions marketplace | Free, lightweight, MAUI support via extensions |
| **Visual Studio for Mac (discontinued)** | — | No longer supported; do **not** install |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) | Full MAUI support, paid |

For VS Code, install these extensions: **C# Dev Kit**, **.NET MAUI**.

---

## 6. Verify Everything

```bash
# 1. Check .NET SDK
dotnet --version
dotnet --list-sdks

# 2. Check workloads
dotnet workload list
# Should show: maui, android, ios, maccatalyst

# 3. Check Xcode
xcodebuild -version
xcrun simctl list devices   # list iOS simulators

# 4. Create and build a test app
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp

# Build for iOS (simulator)
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# 5. Run on iOS Simulator
dotnet run -f net8.0-ios

# 6. Run on Android Emulator (start one first via Android Studio or AVD)
dotnet run -f net8.0-android
```

If using .NET 9, replace `net8.0-*` with `net9.0-*`.

---

## 7. Troubleshooting Checklist

| Symptom | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| `xcode-select: error` | `sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app` |
| Android SDK not found | Set `ANDROID_HOME=~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` |
| Workload restore fails | `sudo dotnet workload repair` |
| Outdated workloads | `sudo dotnet workload update` |
| iOS signing errors (device) | Configure a dev team in `.csproj`: `<CodesignKey>` + Apple Developer account |

---

**TL;DR install order:** Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → VS Code + extensions → verify with `dotnet new maui`.
